libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No environment or system property setup is required. The reported failure is API drift from 2.0.6 to 2.0.7, not missing setup: ArtifactResolutionException and ArtifactNotFoundException constructor signatures changed, so generated tests should use the 2.0.7 signatures or Artifact-based constructors. Prefer isolated/unit-style Maven core tests and side-effect-free MavenCli paths such as --version; if invoking real goals, use temporary directories/settings to avoid depending on the developer machine's ~/.m2 or network state.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8771 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.maven:maven-core:2.0.7; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Full Maven execution can read user settings/local repository and may try plugin or artifact resolution, making tests flaky if they rely on the host environment or network.
Several Maven 2 APIs are old internal-style APIs with version-specific constructors, so tests copied from 2.0.6 may not compile unchanged against 2.0.7.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No concrete extra setup is needed: org.apache.maven:maven-core:2.0.7 resolves its Maven/Plexus/Wagon/ClassWorlds runtime through normal transitive dependencies, and meaningful coverage can be reached through side-effect-free Maven core APIs and MavenCli usage without Docker, extra Maven dependencies, or required environment/system properties.
